basic.q: Choose the correct conjunction: 'I like apples ___ I do not like bananas.'
Identify the conjunction in the sentence: 'She wants to go, but she is tired.'
Fill in the blank: 'Do you want tea ___ coffee?'
Choose the correct option: 'He was hungry, ___ he ate a sandwich.'
Identify the conjunction: 'I went to the store because I needed milk.'
Complete the sentence: 'Cats can run ___ dogs can bark.'
Choose the correct conjunction: 'It was raining, ___ we stayed indoors.'
Find the conjunction in: 'You can have the red toy or the blue toy.'
Fill in the blank: 'She can sing ___ she cannot dance.'
Choose the correct option: 'I missed the bus ___ I woke up late.'
Identify the conjunction: 'Tom and Jerry are good friends.'
Complete the sentence: 'Work hard ___ you will fail.'
Choose the correct conjunction: 'He is small ___ he is very strong.'
Identify the conjunction in: 'We stayed home because it was snowing.'
Fill in the blank: 'Do your homework ___ go play outside.'
Choose the correct option: 'The water was cold, ___ he did not swim.'
Identify the conjunction: 'Is your birthday in May or June?'
Complete the sentence: 'She studies hard ___ she wants to pass.'
Choose the correct conjunction: 'I tried to open the door, ___ it was locked.'
Find the conjunction: 'He likes football and cricket.'
Fill in the blank: 'Wear your jacket ___ it is cold outside.'
Choose the correct option: 'She washed her hands ___ ate dinner.'
Identify the conjunction: 'He ran fast, but he missed the train.'
Complete the sentence: 'You can have juice ___ milk, but not both.'
Choose the correct conjunction: 'The sun set ___ it became dark.'
Identify the conjunction in: 'I was tired, so I went to sleep early.'
Fill in the blank: 'She bought a pen ___ a notebook.'
Choose the correct option: 'He didn't study, ___ he failed the test.'
Identify the conjunction: 'Will you walk or take the bus?'
Complete the sentence: 'The baby cried ___ she was hungry.'

advanced.q: Choose the correct correlative conjunction pair: '___ he works hard, he will not succeed.'
Identify the subordinating conjunction in: 'Although it was raining, they went for a hike.'
Fill in the blank with the correct correlative pair: 'He is ___ smart ___ hardworking.'
Choose the correct coordinating conjunction: 'She must apologize, ___ she will be fired.'
Identify the conjunction type for 'unless', 'since', and 'whereas':
Complete the sentence: 'Hardly had I stepped outside ___ it started to pour.'
Choose the correct option: 'No sooner had she arrived ___ the phone rang.'
Identify the conjunction in: 'You will not pass the exam unless you study daily.'
Fill in the blank: 'She acts ___ she owns the place.'
Choose the correct correlative conjunction: '___ you apologize, I will never speak to you again.'
Identify the conjunctive adverb used as a transition: 'It was late; ___, we decided to leave.'
Complete the sentence: 'He speaks ___ English ___ French fluently.'
Choose the correct conjunction: 'Take a jacket with you ___ it gets cold at night.'
Identify the subordinating conjunction of concession:
Fill in the blank: '___ he is rich, he is very humble.'
Choose the correct pair: '___ the manager ___ the employees agreed on the new policy.'
Identify the conjunction in: 'We saved money so that we could buy a house.'
Complete the sentence: 'She is ___ intelligent ___ she can solve any puzzle.'
Choose the correct option: 'I will support you ___ what happens.'
Identify the compound conjunction: 'We must finish the project ___ the end of the week.'
Fill in the blank: 'He talks ___ he knows everything.'
Choose the correct coordinating conjunction expressing contrast: 'The weather was stormy, ___ they went sailing anyway.'
Identify the subordinating conjunction of time in: 'Call me as soon as you reach home.'
Complete the sentence: '___ you finish your chores, you cannot play video games.'
Choose the correct option: 'He didn't only pass the exam, ___ he topped the class.'
Identify the correlative conjunction error or choice: 'She likes ___ coffee ___ tea.'
Fill in the blank: 'Keep quiet ___ you will wake the baby.'
Choose the correct conjunction: '___ it started to rain, we cancelled the picnic.'
Identify the conjunction in: 'Provided that you keep your promise, I will help you.'
Complete the sentence: 'She practiced intensely ___ she could win the championship.'
